patch_description: This evolution block replaces the previous per-qubit RY pattern with a symmetric three-group RY layer and a shared-parameter, globally entangling CRZ layer per block. The wires are partitioned into three groups (0,3,6) -> a; (1,4,7) -> b; (2,5,8) -> c, enabling parameter sharing across qubits to improve parameter efficiency while still providing expressive power. After the re-uploaded data encoding, each block performs a CZ entangling layer on all admissible grid edges, followed by a CRZ layer across the same set of edges that uses a single shared parameter crz for all CRZ gates within the block. This design aims to increase validation/test accuracy and reduce the train-test gap while keeping the same overall training loop, data, and measurement setup. The new ANSATZ_SPEC preserves the same inputs/outputs and adheres to all connectivity and gate-type constraints.
